prerequisites: An active NetSuite account with the SuiteAnalytics Connect feature enabled and a role that holds the SuiteAnalytics Connect permission.

The Service Host, Service Port (typically 1708), Account ID (account_id) and Role ID (role_id) for the Connect Service, found on the SuiteAnalytics Connect Driver Download page under Your Configuration.
A NetSuite username with an active employee record (passwords must not be expired and 'Require Password Change on Next Login' must not be checked).
For OAuth 2.0 or Token-Based Authentication, the role must have access to the NetSuite2.com data source.
Optional: an SSH bastion host and key or password if connecting through an SSH tunnel (tunnel_method).

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (client credentials / machine-to-machine)
authMethods.summary: Maps to authentication_method.oauth2_private_key and authentication_method.client_secret. Create an integration record (Setup > Integration > Manage Integrations), check SuiteAnalytics Connect on the OAuth 2.0 subtab, select the Client Credentials grant, and upload a public key certificate. Supply the resulting client secret and the matching private key. Requires the 'Log in Using OAuth 2.0 Access Tokens' permission and supports the NetSuite2.com data source only.

authMethods.name: Token-Based Authentication (TBA)
authMethods.summary: Maps to authentication_method.token_secret. A token-based mechanism for the NetSuite2.com data source. Supply the consumer/token credentials including the token secret. The role must have SuiteAnalytics Connect access and NetSuite2.com data source access.

authMethods.name: Username and Password
authMethods.summary: Maps to authentication_method.password. Authenticate with the NetSuite account email (username) and its password, following the same password policy as the NetSuite UI. Access is denied if the password has expired or the employee record is inactive.

setupSteps.title: Enable SuiteAnalytics Connect and assign the role
setupSteps.body: In NetSuite, ensure the SuiteAnalytics Connect feature is enabled and assign your user a role that holds the SuiteAnalytics Connect permission. For OAuth 2.0 or Token-Based Authentication, the role must also have access to the NetSuite2.com data source. Confirm the employee record is active and the password is not expired.

setupSteps.title: Collect the Connect Service connection details
setupSteps.body: Open the SuiteAnalytics Connect Driver Download page and read the Your Configuration panel. Record the Service Host (host), Service Port (port, typically 1708), Account ID (account_id) and Role ID (role_id). These map directly to the connector's host, port, account_id and role_id fields. The JDBC URL takes the form jdbc:ns://<host>:<port>;ServerDataSource=NetSuite2.com;CustomProperties=(AccountID=<account_id>;RoleID=<role_id>).

setupSteps.title: Create credentials for your chosen auth method
setupSteps.body: For OAuth 2.0, create an integration record under Setup > Integration > Manage Integrations, check SuiteAnalytics Connect on the OAuth 2.0 subtab, choose the Client Credentials grant, upload your public key certificate, and save the client secret; provide the matching private key. For Token-Based Authentication, generate the token and record its token secret. For username and password, use your NetSuite login email and password.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set authentication_method to your chosen method and provide its secret (oauth2_private_key + client_secret, token_secret, or password). Enter host, port, account_id, role_id and username. Set the cursor field used for incremental sync. Optionally set jdbc_url_params, concurrency, checkpoint_target_interval_seconds, and check_privileges. If reaching NetSuite through a bastion, set tunnel_method and supply the SSH key or tunnel user password.
